q: Energy is the basic ___ for life.
Energy demand is the term used to describe the consumption of energy by ___ activities.
Energy demand forecasting is crucial for ensuring future economic growth and ___ security.
Supply of energy is, therefore, ___ than the actual demand.
The energy ___ is the concern that the world's demands on the limited natural resources are diminishing as the demand rises.
With ninety percent of the world's oil reserves already ___, people need to find new ways to make energy.
Energy demand depends on socioeconomic factors such as population, urbanization, and ___.
A ___ resource is a natural substance that is not replenished with the speed at which it is consumed.
Non-renewable energy resources include coal, natural gas, crude oil, and ___ energy.
Renewable energy sources are also called ___-conventional energy sources.
Renewable energy can be generated at a ___ rate for their use over and over again.
Renewable sources can be produced at a rate greater than or equal to the rate of their ___.
___ resources are replenished naturally and over short periods of time.
___ is the main constituent of these fossil fuels.
The fossil fuel industry extracts ___ oil from the ground and converts it to gasoline.
The process of coal formation is called ___.
Coal is not a ___ substance; its composition varies from deposit to deposit.
Natural gas always contains some gases, but this natural gas (___) does not take nearly as long to form.
Methane is a common gas found in ___ and is also the by-product of animals' digestive system.
Nuclear energy is non-renewable as the ___ fuels used are consumed in the nuclear fission reaction.
___ obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions are used in medicine, agriculture and research.
Solar energy is the radiant ___ and ___ from the sun.
The total amount of radiation energy passing through per unit area and per unit time is called ___.
Irradiance is measured in $W \cdot m^{-2}$ or ___.
Another commonly used term for irradiance of sunlight is ___.
The solar radiation intensity above the earth's atmosphere is known as the ___.
Conversion of light energy into electrical energy is based on a phenomenon called ___ effect.
A solar cell is basically a ___-junction diode.
Individual solar cells can be combined to form modules commonly known as ___.
Power conversion efficiency of the solar panel is the ratio of the electrical power delivered by the panel to the solar power ___ by it.
Flat plate collectors have ___ pipes attached to an absorber plate.
Concentrated solar power (CSP) systems generate solar power by using ___ or lenses.
A wind turbine converts wind energy into ___, which can then be used to power electrical equipment.
The amount of power that can be harvested from wind depends on the size of the ___ and the length of its blades.
The output of a wind turbine is proportional to the ___ of the wind speed.
Hydroelectric energy harnesses the power of water in ___.
A ___ is a closed conduit that channels the flow of water to turbines with water flow regulated by gates and valves.
Tidal energy is produced by the gravitational pull of mainly the ___ on the spinning earth.
Tidal energy is harnessed by constructing a dam (a tidal ___) across a narrow opening to the sea.
Ocean thermal-energy conversion plants exploit the difference in ___ to obtain energy.
Geothermal energy is a type of renewable energy taken from the Earth's ___.
Thermal energy from ___ can be used to generate electricity.
___ is renewable organic material that comes from plants and animals.
Biomass gasification produces a carbon monoxide and hydrogen rich gas called synthesis gas or ___.
The chemical conversion process known as ___ is used for converting vegetable oils into biodiesel.
Biological conversion includes fermentation to convert biomass into ___.
Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's ___.
Main greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide ($CO_2$), ___ ($CH_4$), and oxides of nitrogen ($NO_x$).
___ also destroy the earth's protective ozone layer which shields the earth from harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of ___ and oxides of nitrogen.
Acidified clouds could travel great ___ before releasing the acid rain.
The oil industry adds ___ ($Pb$) to petrol to help engines run more smoothly.
Smoke from the combustion of leaded fuels causes cancer and high ___ pressure in adults.
Leaded fumes reduce intelligence in children, thus hindering ___ ability.
Excavated areas for coal mining that are not filled and revegetated cause ___ pollution.
Low frequency sound and ___ are usually beneath the threshold of human hearing.
Wind turbines may create ___ flicker on nearby residences.
Large dams are breeding grounds for ___ and cause the spread of disease.
Dams serve as a ___ sink, where water in dams is hotter than normal river water.
Geothermal power is a relatively ___ (less harmful) source of energy.
Biomass power plants require ___ for cooling, like coal and natural gas-fired power plants.
In wind energy transfer, ___ energy is converted to electrical energy.
For bio-fuel, chemical energy $\rightarrow$ thermal energy $\rightarrow$ ___ energy $\rightarrow$ electrical energy.
In hydroelectricity energy transfer, ___ energy is converted into kinetic energy.
